         - Default Docker daemon config is sufficient.  
         - Verify Docker daemon is running and accessible.  
     **Docker Image Build and Verification**  
     12. Build Docker image using adjusted Dockerfile (no CUDA base):  
         ```
         docker build . -t pluralis_node0
         ```  
         - Dockerfile installs Python 3.11, dependencies via `pip install .`, and node0 package.  
     13. Verify container environment:  
         ```
         docker run --rm -it pluralis_node0 bash
         ```  
         - Check Python version: `python --version` (≥3.11,<3.12)  
         - Check node0 package installed: `pip show node0`  
         - Check locale: `echo $LC_ALL` → `en_US.UTF-8`  
         - GPU commands such as `nvidia-smi` are not applicable here and should be skipped.  
     **Authorization & Script Generation**  
     14. Obtain HuggingFace token.  
     15. Generate `start_server.sh`:  
         - With Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --use_docker --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
     16. Verify `start_server.sh` is created.  
     **Networking Setup**  
     17. Ensure port 49200 or configured port is open inbound and outbound.  
     18. Modify port parameters if needed in `start_server.sh`.  
     19. Test port accessibility externally.  
     **Run & Monitor**  
     20. Start server:  
         ```
         ./start_server.sh
         ```  
     21. Monitor logs:  
         - Docker mode:  
           ```
           tail -f run.out
           ```  
         - Non-Docker mode: run in `tmux` and monitor `logs/server.log`.  
     22. Verify authorization success and batch processing via logs.  
     23. Confirm no critical errors in logs.  
     **Stopping & Cleanup**  
     24. Stop server gracefully:  
         - Docker:  
           ```
           docker ps
           docker stop <container_name_or_ID>
           docker rm <container_name_or_ID>
           sudo chown -R <linux_user> .
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           - Press `Ctrl + Z`  
           - Remove socket files:  
             ```
             rm /tmp/hivemind*
             ```  
           - Kill processes on port 49200:  
             ```
             sudo apt-get install lsof
             for i in $(sudo lsof -t -i tcp:49200); do kill -9 $i; done
             ```  
     25. Preserve `private.key` between runs.  
     **Error Handling & Verification**  
     26. Authorization failures: check token, delete `private.key` and retry.  
     27. Network errors: verify port forwarding, firewall, internet stability.  
     28. Docker issues: verify Docker service, restart if needed.  
     29. Restart machine if persistent issues.  
     **Optional: Multi-Instance Setup**  
     30. For multiple instances:  
         - Clone repo into separate folders.  
         - Generate `start_server.sh` with distinct `--host_port` and `--announce_port`.  
         - Run independently.  
     31. Monitor logs and resource usage per instance.  
     **Documentation & References**

  - **Skip Docker daemon NVIDIA runtime configuration.**  
  - **Skip GPU detection and CUDA runtime tests.**  
  - **Ensure all paths and volumes respect nested working directory structure.**  
  - **Maintain Python version and dependency installation as per `pyproject.toml`.**  
  - **Continue using `hatchling` as build backend and install dependencies via `pip install .`.**  
  - **Keep general instructions for authorization, networking, running, stopping, and troubleshooting unchanged except GPU-specific parts removed.**  
  This adjusted plan ensures compatibility and smooth setup on your CPU-only x86_64 Linux environment without NVIDIA GPUs.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:22.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  ENV LANG=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE=en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
  RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      software-properties-common \
      build-essential \
      curl \
      wget \
      git \
      vim \
      rsync \
      openssh-client \
      jq \
      lsof \
      locales \
      ca-certificates \
      libffi-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libpq-dev \
      pkg-config \
      cmake \
      libprotobuf-dev \
      protobuf-compiler \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
      libreadline-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libsqlite3-dev \
      libncurses5-dev \
      libncursesw5-dev \
      python3.11 \
      python3.11-venv \
      python3.11-distutils \
      python3.11-dev \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  RUN curl -sS https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py | python3.11
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install hatchling
  WORKDIR /root/node0
  COPY . .
  RUN curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh -s -- -y
  ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
  RUN rustup default stable
  RUN rustc --version && cargo --version && rustup show
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --verbose git+https://github.com/learning-at-home/hivemind.git
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install . --verbose
  CMD ["/bin/bash"]

         - Default Docker daemon config is sufficient.  
         - Verify Docker daemon is running and accessible.  
     **Docker Image Build and Verification**  
     12. Build Docker image using adjusted Dockerfile (no CUDA base):  
         ```
         docker build . -t pluralis_node0
         ```  
         - Dockerfile installs Python 3.11, dependencies via `pip install .`, and node0 package.  
     13. Verify container environment:  
         ```
         docker run --rm -it pluralis_node0 bash
         ```  
         - Check Python version: `python --version` (≥3.11,<3.12)  
         - Check node0 package installed: `pip show node0`  
         - Check locale: `echo $LC_ALL` → `en_US.UTF-8`  
         - GPU commands such as `nvidia-smi` are not applicable here and should be skipped.  
     **Authorization & Script Generation**  
     14. Obtain HuggingFace token.  
     15. Generate `start_server.sh`:  
         - With Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --use_docker --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
     16. Verify `start_server.sh` is created.  
     **Networking Setup**  
     17. Ensure port 49200 or configured port is open inbound and outbound.  
     18. Modify port parameters if needed in `start_server.sh`.  
     19. Test port accessibility externally.

         ```
         ./start_server.sh
         ```  
     21. Monitor logs:  
         - Docker mode:  
           ```
           tail -f run.out
           ```  
         - Non-Docker mode: run in `tmux` and monitor `logs/server.log`.  
     22. Verify authorization success and batch processing via logs.  
     23. Confirm no critical errors in logs.  
     **Stopping & Cleanup**  
     24. Stop server gracefully:  
         - Docker:  
           ```
           docker ps
           docker stop <container_name_or_ID>
           docker rm <container_name_or_ID>
           sudo chown -R <linux_user> .
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           - Press `Ctrl + Z`  
           - Remove socket files:  
             ```
             rm /tmp/hivemind*
             ```  
           - Kill processes on port 49200:  
             ```
             sudo apt-get install lsof
             for i in $(sudo lsof -t -i tcp:49200); do kill -9 $i; done
             ```  
     25. Preserve `private.key` between runs.  
     **Error Handling & Verification**  
     26. Authorization failures: check token, delete `private.key` and retry.  
     27. Network errors: verify port forwarding, firewall, internet stability.  
     28. Docker issues: verify Docker service, restart if needed.  
     29. Restart machine if persistent issues.  
     **Optional: Multi-Instance Setup**  
     30. For multiple instances:  
         - Clone repo into separate folders.  
         - Generate `start_server.sh` with distinct `--host_port` and `--announce_port`.  
         - Run independently.  
     31. Monitor logs and resource usage per instance.  
     **Documentation & References**

  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install hatchling
  WORKDIR /root/node0
  COPY . .
  RUN curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh -s -- -y
  ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
  RUN rustup default stable
  RUN rustc --version && cargo --version && rustup show
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --verbose git+https://github.com/learning-at-home/hivemind.git
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install . --verbose
  CMD ["/bin/bash"]

        ```

     **Docker Daemon Configuration (Linux only)**  
     11. **Skip NVIDIA runtime configuration steps.**  
         - Default Docker daemon config is sufficient.  
         - Verify Docker daemon is running and accessible.  
     **Docker Image Build and Verification**  
     12. Build Docker image using adjusted Dockerfile (no CUDA base):  
         ```
         docker build . -t pluralis_node0
         ```  
         - Dockerfile installs Python 3.11, dependencies via `pip install .`, and node0 package.  
     13. Verify container environment:  
         ```
         docker run --rm -it pluralis_node0 bash
         ```  
         - Check Python version: `python --version` (≥3.11,<3.12)  
         - Check node0 package installed: `pip show node0`  
         - Check locale: `echo $LC_ALL` → `en_US.UTF-8`  
         - GPU commands such as `nvidia-smi` are not applicable here and should be skipped.  
     **Authorization & Script Generation**  
     14. Obtain HuggingFace token.  
     15. Generate `start_server.sh`:  
         - With Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --use_docker --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
     16. Verify `start_server.sh` is created.  
     **Networking Setup**  
     17. Ensure port 49200 or configured port is open inbound and outbound.  
     18. Modify port parameters if needed in `start_server.sh`.  
     19. Test port accessibility externally.  
     **Run & Monitor**  
     20. Start server:  
         ```
         ./start_server.sh
         ```  
     21. Monitor logs:  
         - Docker mode:  
           ```
           tail -f run.out
           ```  
         - Non-Docker mode: run in `tmux` and monitor `logs/server.log`.  
     22. Verify authorization success and batch processing via logs.  
     23. Confirm no critical errors in logs.  
     **Stopping & Cleanup**  
     24. Stop server gracefully:  
         - Docker:  
           ```
           docker ps
           docker stop <container_name_or_ID>
           docker rm <container_name_or_ID>
           sudo chown -R <linux_user> .
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           - Press `Ctrl + Z`  
           - Remove socket files:  
             ```
             rm /tmp/hivemind*
             ```  
           - Kill processes on port 49200:  
             ```
             sudo apt-get install lsof
             for i in $(sudo lsof -t -i tcp:49200); do kill -9 $i; done
             ```  
     25. Preserve `private.key` between runs.  
     **Error Handling & Verification**  
     26. Authorization failures: check token, delete `private.key` and retry.  
     27. Network errors: verify port forwarding, firewall, internet stability.  
     28. Docker issues: verify Docker service, restart if needed.  
     29. Restart machine if persistent issues.  
     **Optional: Multi-Instance Setup**  
     30. For multiple instances:  
         - Clone repo into separate folders.  
         - Generate `start_server.sh` with distinct `--host_port` and `--announce_port`.  
         - Run independently.  
     31. Monitor logs and resource usage per instance.  
     **Documentation & References**  
     32. Refer to `docs/useful_links.md`, `docs/network.md`, `docs/cloud.md`.  
     33. Keep `README.md` and citation info for attribution.

  **Summary of Main Adjustments:**  
  - **Remove all GPU-related requirements and steps:** No NVIDIA drivers, CUDA toolkit, or nvidia-docker2.  
  - **Use standard Linux amd64 base Docker image (e.g., ubuntu:20.04) instead of CUDA base image.**  
  - **Skip Docker daemon NVIDIA runtime configuration.**  
  - **Skip GPU detection and CUDA runtime tests.**  
  - **Ensure all paths and volumes respect nested working directory structure.**  
  - **Maintain Python version and dependency installation as per `pyproject.toml`.**  
  - **Continue using `hatchling` as build backend and install dependencies via `pip install .`.**  
  - **Keep general instructions for authorization, networking, running, stopping, and troubleshooting unchanged except GPU-specific parts removed.**  
  This adjusted plan ensures compatibility and smooth setup on your CPU-only x86_64 Linux environment without NVIDIA GPUs.

  #16 3.992           To prevent this problem, you can list `dependencies` under `dynamic` or alternatively
  #16 3.992           remove the `[project]` table from your file and rely entirely on other means of
  #16 3.992           configuration.
  #16 3.992           ********************************************************************************
  #16 3.993 
  #16 3.993   !!
  #16 3.993     _handle_missing_dynamic(dist, project_table)
  #16 3.993   /tmp/pip-build-env-j9vxr5c_/overlay/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/setuptools/config/_apply_pyprojecttoml.py:75: _MissingDynamic: `optional-dependencies` defined outside of `pyproject.toml` is ignored.
  #16 3.994   !!
  #16 3.994 
  #16 3.994           ********************************************************************************
  #16 3.994           The following seems to be defined outside of `pyproject.toml`:
  #16 3.994 
  #16 3.995           `optional-dependencies = {'dev': ['pytest==8.3.5', 'pytest-forked', 'pytest-asyncio==0.26.0', 'pytest-cov', 'pytest-timeout', 'coverage', 'tqdm', 'scikit-learn', 'codespell==2.2.2', 'psutil', 'pytest-xdist', 'ruff==0.11.2'], 'docs': ['recommonmark==0.5.0', 'sphinx_rtd_theme==0.4.3', 'docutils==0.16', 'sphinx==5.0.0'], 'bitsandbytes': ['bitsandbytes~=0.45.2'], 'all': ['pytest==8.3.5', 'pytest-forked', 'pytest-asyncio==0.26.0', 'pytest-cov', 'pytest-timeout', 'coverage', 'tqdm', 'scikit-learn', 'codespell==2.2.2', 'psutil', 'pytest-xdist', 'ruff==0.11.2', 'recommonmark==0.5.0', 'sphinx_rtd_theme==0.4.3', 'docutils==0.16', 'sphinx==5.0.0', 'bitsandbytes~=0.45.2']}`

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:22.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  ENV LANG=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE=en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
  ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
  RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      software-properties-common \
      build-essential \
      curl \
      wget \
      git \
      vim \
      rsync \
      openssh-client \
      jq \
      lsof \
      locales \
      ca-certificates \
      libffi-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libpq-dev \
      pkg-config \
      cmake \
      libprotobuf-dev \
      protobuf-compiler \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
      libreadline-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libsqlite3-dev \
      libncurses5-dev \
      libncursesw5-dev \
      python3.11 \
      python3.11-venv \
      python3.11-distutils \
      python3.11-dev \
      cargo \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  RUN curl -sS https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py | python3.11
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install hatchling
  WORKDIR /root/node0
  COPY . .
  RUN curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh -s -- -y
  RUN rustup default stable
  RUN rustc --version && cargo --version && rustup show
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --verbose --upgrade git+https://github.com/learning-at-home/hivemind.git
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install . --verbose
  CMD ["/bin/bash"]

        ```  
     **Repository Setup**

        ```
        git clone https://github.com/PluralisResearch/node0
        cd node0
        ```

     8. Create and activate conda environment for node0:

        ```

        pip install .
        ```

     **Docker Daemon Configuration (Linux only)**  
     11. **Skip NVIDIA runtime configuration steps.**  
         - Default Docker daemon config is sufficient.  
         - Verify Docker daemon is running and accessible.  
     **Docker Image Build and Verification**  
     12. Build Docker image using adjusted Dockerfile (no CUDA base):  
         ```
         docker build . -t pluralis_node0
         ```  
         - Dockerfile installs Python 3.11, dependencies via `pip install .`, and node0 package.  
     13. Verify container environment:  
         ```
         docker run --rm -it pluralis_node0 bash
         ```  
         - Check Python version: `python --version` (≥3.11,<3.12)  
         - Check node0 package installed: `pip show node0`  
         - Check locale: `echo $LC_ALL` → `en_US.UTF-8`  
         - GPU commands such as `nvidia-smi` are not applicable here and should be skipped.  
     **Authorization & Script Generation**  
     14. Obtain HuggingFace token.  
     15. Generate `start_server.sh`:  
         - With Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --use_docker --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
     16. Verify `start_server.sh` is created.  
     **Networking Setup**  
     17. Ensure port 49200 or configured port is open inbound and outbound.  
     18. Modify port parameters if needed in `start_server.sh`.  
     19. Test port accessibility externally.  
     **Run & Monitor**  
     20. Start server:  
         ```
         ./start_server.sh
         ```  
     21. Monitor logs:  
         - Docker mode:  
           ```
           tail -f run.out
           ```  
         - Non-Docker mode: run in `tmux` and monitor `logs/server.log`.  
     22. Verify authorization success and batch processing via logs.  
     23. Confirm no critical errors in logs.  
     **Stopping & Cleanup**  
     24. Stop server gracefully:  
         - Docker:  
           ```
           docker ps
           docker stop <container_name_or_ID>
           docker rm <container_name_or_ID>
           sudo chown -R <linux_user> .
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           - Press `Ctrl + Z`  
           - Remove socket files:  
             ```
             rm /tmp/hivemind*
             ```  
           - Kill processes on port 49200:  
             ```
             sudo apt-get install lsof
             for i in $(sudo lsof -t -i tcp:49200); do kill -9 $i; done
             ```  
     25. Preserve `private.key` between runs.  
     **Error Handling & Verification**  
     26. Authorization failures: check token, delete `private.key` and retry.  
     27. Network errors: verify port forwarding, firewall, internet stability.  
     28. Docker issues: verify Docker service, restart if needed.  
     29. Restart machine if persistent issues.  
     **Optional: Multi-Instance Setup**  
     30. For multiple instances:  
         - Clone repo into separate folders.  
         - Generate `start_server.sh` with distinct `--host_port` and `--announce_port`.  
         - Run independently.  
     31. Monitor logs and resource usage per instance.  
     **Documentation & References**  
     32. Refer to `docs/useful_links.md`, `docs/network.md`, `docs/cloud.md`.  
     33. Keep `README.md` and citation info for attribution.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:22.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  ENV LANG=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE=en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
  ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
  RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      software-properties-common \
      build-essential \
      curl \
      wget \
      git \
      vim \
      rsync \
      openssh-client \
      jq \
      lsof \
      locales \
      ca-certificates \
      libffi-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libpq-dev \
      pkg-config \
      cmake \
      libprotobuf-dev \
      protobuf-compiler \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
      libreadline-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libsqlite3-dev \
      libncurses5-dev \
      libncursesw5-dev \
      python3.11 \
      python3.11-venv \
      python3.11-distutils \
      python3.11-dev \
      cargo \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  RUN curl -sS https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py | python3.11
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install hatchling
  WORKDIR /root/node0
  COPY . .
  RUN curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh -s -- -y
  ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
  RUN rustup default stable
  RUN rustc --version && cargo --version && rustup show
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --verbose --upgrade git+https://github.com/learning-at-home/hivemind.git@v0.7.44
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install . --verbose
  CMD ["/bin/bash"]

  4. **COMPLETE TODO LIST:**  
     **Preparation**  
     1. Verify hardware meets minimum requirements:  
        - Since no GPU, verify sufficient CPU and RAM (≥32GB recommended) and ≥80GB disk space available.  
        - GPU minimum requirement omitted.  
     2. Choose OS environment: Linux native preferred (x86_64).  
     3. **Skip GPU drivers and CUDA Toolkit installation entirely.**  
     4. Install Docker only (no NVIDIA Container Toolkit).  
     5. Install Git, Python 3.11 (≥3.11,<3.12), Conda (optional), jq, lsof, tmux (optional).  
     6. Install `hatchling` build backend system-wide or in base environment:  
        ```
        pip install hatchling
        ```  
     **Repository Setup**  
     7. Clone repository:  
        ```
        git clone https://github.com/PluralisResearch/node0
        cd node0
        ```  
     **Python Environment Setup (From Source Installation)**  
     8. Create and activate conda environment for node0:  
        ```
        conda create -n node0 python=3.11
        conda activate node0
        ```  
     9. Install package dependencies using `pyproject.toml`:  
        ```
        pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install .
        ```  
     10. Verify Python environment:  
         - Check Python version (`python --version`) is within `>=3.11,<3.12`  
         - Confirm `node0` package installed (`pip show node0`)  
     **Docker Daemon Configuration (Linux only)**  
     11. **Skip NVIDIA runtime configuration steps.**  
         - Default Docker daemon config is sufficient.  
         - Verify Docker daemon is running and accessible.  
     **Docker Image Build and Verification**  
     12. Build Docker image using adjusted Dockerfile (no CUDA base):  
         ```
         docker build . -t pluralis_node0
         ```  
         - Dockerfile installs Python 3.11, dependencies via `pip install .`, and node0 package.  
     13. Verify container environment:  
         ```
         docker run --rm -it pluralis_node0 bash
         ```  
         - Check Python version: `python --version` (≥3.11,<3.12)  
         - Check node0 package installed: `pip show node0`  
         - Check locale: `echo $LC_ALL` → `en_US.UTF-8`  
         - GPU commands such as `nvidia-smi` are not applicable here and should be skipped.  
     **Authorization & Script Generation**  
     14. Obtain HuggingFace token.  
     15. Generate `start_server.sh`:  
         - With Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --use_docker --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
     16. Verify `start_server.sh` is created.  
     **Networking Setup**  
     17. Ensure port 49200 or configured port is open inbound and outbound.  
     18. Modify port parameters if needed in `start_server.sh`.  
     19. Test port accessibility externally.  
     **Run & Monitor**  
     20. Start server:  
         ```
         ./start_server.sh
         ```  
     21. Monitor logs:  
         - Docker mode:  
           ```
           tail -f run.out
           ```  
         - Non-Docker mode: run in `tmux` and monitor `logs/server.log`.  
     22. Verify authorization success and batch processing via logs.  
     23. Confirm no critical errors in logs.  
     **Stopping & Cleanup**  
     24. Stop server gracefully:  
         - Docker:  
           ```
           docker ps
           docker stop <container_name_or_ID>
           docker rm <container_name_or_ID>
           sudo chown -R <linux_user> .
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           - Press `Ctrl + Z`  
           - Remove socket files:  
             ```
             rm /tmp/hivemind*
             ```  
           - Kill processes on port 49200:  
             ```
             sudo apt-get install lsof
             for i in $(sudo lsof -t -i tcp:49200); do kill -9 $i; done
             ```  
     25. Preserve `private.key` between runs.  
     **Error Handling & Verification**  
     26. Authorization failures: check token, delete `private.key` and retry.  
     27. Network errors: verify port forwarding, firewall, internet stability.  
     28. Docker issues: verify Docker service, restart if needed.  
     29. Restart machine if persistent issues.  
     **Optional: Multi-Instance Setup**  
     30. For multiple instances:  
         - Clone repo into separate folders.  
         - Generate `start_server.sh` with distinct `--host_port` and `--announce_port`.  
         - Run independently.  
     31. Monitor logs and resource usage per instance.  
     **Documentation & References**  
     32. Refer to `docs/useful_links.md`, `docs/network.md`, `docs/cloud.md`.  
     33. Keep `README.md` and citation info for attribution.

  This adjusted plan ensures compatibility and smooth setup on your CPU-only x86_64 Linux environment without NVIDIA GPUs.

  4. **COMPLETE TODO LIST:**  
     **Preparation**  
     1. Verify hardware meets minimum requirements:  
        - Since no GPU, verify sufficient CPU and RAM (≥32GB recommended) and ≥80GB disk space available.  
        - GPU minimum requirement omitted.  
     2. Choose OS environment: Linux native preferred (x86_64).  
     3. **Skip GPU drivers and CUDA Toolkit installation entirely.**  
     4. Install Docker only (no NVIDIA Container Toolkit).  
     5. Install Git, Python 3.11 (≥3.11,<3.12), Conda (optional), jq, lsof, tmux (optional).  
     6. Install `hatchling` build backend system-wide or in base environment:  
        ```
        pip install hatchling
        ```  
     **Repository Setup**  
     7. Clone repository:  
        ```
        git clone https://github.com/PluralisResearch/node0
        cd node0
        ```  
     **Python Environment Setup (From Source Installation)**  
     8. Create and activate conda environment for node0:  
        ```
        conda create -n node0 python=3.11
        conda activate node0
        ```  
     9. Install package dependencies using `pyproject.toml`:  
        ```
        pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install .
        ```  
     10. Verify Python environment:  
         - Check Python version (`python --version`) is within `>=3.11,<3.12`  
         - Confirm `node0` package installed (`pip show node0`)  
     **Docker Daemon Configuration (Linux only)**  
     11. **Skip NVIDIA runtime configuration steps.**  
         - Default Docker daemon config is sufficient.  
         - Verify Docker daemon is running and accessible.  
     **Docker Image Build and Verification**  
     12. Build Docker image using adjusted Dockerfile (no CUDA base):  
         ```
         docker build . -t pluralis_node0
         ```  
         - Dockerfile installs Python 3.11, dependencies via `pip install .`, and node0 package.  
     13. Verify container environment:  
         ```
         docker run --rm -it pluralis_node0 bash
         ```  
         - Check Python version: `python --version` (≥3.11,<3.12)  
         - Check node0 package installed: `pip show node0`  
         - Check locale: `echo $LC_ALL` → `en_US.UTF-8`  
         - GPU commands such as `nvidia-smi` are not applicable here and should be skipped.  
     **Authorization & Script Generation**  
     14. Obtain HuggingFace token.  
     15. Generate `start_server.sh`:  
         - With Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --use_docker --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           ```
           python3 generate_script.py --token <HF_token> [--email <youremail>] [--skip_input]
           ```  
     16. Verify `start_server.sh` is created.  
     **Networking Setup**  
     17. Ensure port 49200 or configured port is open inbound and outbound.  
     18. Modify port parameters if needed in `start_server.sh`.  
     19. Test port accessibility externally.  
     **Run & Monitor**  
     20. Start server:  
         ```
         ./start_server.sh
         ```  
     21. Monitor logs:  
         - Docker mode:  
           ```
           tail -f run.out
           ```  
         - Non-Docker mode: run in `tmux` and monitor `logs/server.log`.  
     22. Verify authorization success and batch processing via logs.  
     23. Confirm no critical errors in logs.  
     **Stopping & Cleanup**  
     24. Stop server gracefully:  
         - Docker:  
           ```
           docker ps
           docker stop <container_name_or_ID>
           docker rm <container_name_or_ID>
           sudo chown -R <linux_user> .
           ```  
         - Without Docker:  
           - Press `Ctrl + Z`  
           - Remove socket files:  
             ```
             rm /tmp/hivemind*
             ```  
           - Kill processes on port 49200:  
             ```
             sudo apt-get install lsof
             for i in $(sudo lsof -t -i tcp:49200); do kill -9 $i; done
             ```  
     25. Preserve `private.key` between runs.  
     **Error Handling & Verification**  
     26. Authorization failures: check token, delete `private.key` and retry.  
     27. Network errors: verify port forwarding, firewall, internet stability.  
     28. Docker issues: verify Docker service, restart if needed.  
     29. Restart machine if persistent issues.  
     **Optional: Multi-Instance Setup**  
     30. For multiple instances:  
         - Clone repo into separate folders.  
         - Generate `start_server.sh` with distinct `--host_port` and `--announce_port`.  
         - Run independently.  
     31. Monitor logs and resource usage per instance.  
     **Documentation & References**  
     32. Refer to `docs/useful_links.md`, `docs/network.md`, `docs/cloud.md`.  
     33. Keep `README.md` and citation info for attribution.

  - **Skip GPU detection and CUDA runtime tests.**

  - **Continue using `hatchling` as build backend and install dependencies via `pip install .`.**  
  - **Keep general instructions for authorization, networking, running, stopping, and troubleshooting unchanged except GPU-specific parts removed.**  
  This adjusted plan ensures compatibility and smooth setup on your CPU-only x86_64 Linux environment without NVIDIA GPUs.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:22.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  ENV LANG=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE=en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
  ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
  RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      software-properties-common \
      build-essential \
      curl \
      wget \
      git \
      vim \
      rsync \
      openssh-client \
      jq \
      lsof \
      locales \
      ca-certificates \
      libffi-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libpq-dev \
      pkg-config \
      cmake \
      libprotobuf-dev \
      protobuf-compiler \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
      libreadline-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libsqlite3-dev \
      libncurses5-dev \
      libncursesw5-dev \
      python3.11 \
      python3.11-venv \
      python3.11-distutils \
      python3.11-dev \
      cargo \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  RUN curl -sS https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py | python3.11
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install hatchling
  WORKDIR /root/node0
  COPY . .
  RUN curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh -s -- -y
  ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
  RUN rustup default stable
  RUN rustc --version && cargo --version && rustup show
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install --upgrade git+https://github.com/learning-at-home/hivemind.git@v0.7.44
  RUN python3.11 -m pip install . --verbose
  CMD ["/bin/bash"]
